capabilities.description: Resolves .strm placeholders to live streams at playback time, and serves Wisp's management UI.
Reports newly written .strm placeholders to Silo's autoscan, so requested items appear without a full library scan.
Turns a Silo request into a playable placeholder immediately, resolved on demand at playback.
Writes placeholders for episodes that have aired since the last pass. Runs at startup by default; set a schedule under the plugin's task settings to have it run periodically.

fields.label: AIOStreams URL
fields.description: Your full AIOStreams manifest URL, copied from its configure page. It carries everything Wisp needs — there is no password to set. Must be the full form containing your configuration.

fields.label: Library Path
fields.description: Directory where Wisp writes .strm placeholders. Must be writable by Silo. Wisp creates movies/, tv/, anime/movies/ and anime/shows/ under it — add a Silo library for each root you want to use.

presentation.summary: On-demand playback: requests become instant library placeholders, resolved to a real stream the moment someone presses play.
presentation.descriptionMarkdown:
Wisp turns a request into a playable library item immediately. It writes a small `.strm` placeholder into your library, Silo picks it up on the next scan, and the actual stream is resolved through AIOStreams only when playback begins.

Because nothing durable ever stores a stream URL, expiring debrid links stop being a problem: every play resolves fresh. There is no mount, no FUSE, and no privileged container — a placeholder is an ordinary text file.

Requires a Silo build with `.strm` support.
presentation.setupMarkdown:
1. Install the plugin.
2. Set the AIOStreams URL and password, and the library path Wisp should write placeholders into.
3. Create a Silo library pointed at that same path.
4. Request something, then run a scan.
